title: Yui Gendo Kozos original plan
wikitext:
[]: Yui/Gendo/Kozo's original plan?             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 4:16 pm Split out from "Main antagonist" . ElMariachi wrote: Wait, where was it said that YGF originally wanted to thwart SEELE?

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 09, 2013 4:26 pm Yes, I see what you mean, I expressed myself very poorly sorry, what I wanted to ask is : YGF want to thwart SEELE's HIP, but to what with it after? Obviously Gendo wasn't aware that his wife's ultimate goal was to become an eternal monument of mankind, so what was exactly their original plan?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 5:08 pm ElMariachi wrote: Yes, I see what you mean, I expressed myself very poorly sorry, what I wanted to ask is : YGF want to thwart SEELE's HIP, but to what with it after? Obviously Gendo wasn't aware that his wife's ultimate goal was to become an eternal monument of mankind, so what was exactly their original plan?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 09, 2013 6:06 pm Reichu wrote: One additional possibility I should mention, while being completely unsure of its validity, is that YGF didn't necessarily see HIP as the enemy, but planned to use it as a tool to benefit humanity. In other words, it would be a means to -- however transiently -- facilitate people's ability to understand the Other.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 09, 2013 6:26 pm We know Yui & pals were explicitly against Seele's goal, but what that goal actually was is very hard to pin down. Remember that instrumentality is something Gendo suggests to them AFTER Yui has been absorbed and Fuyu is suprised by the move, so clearly that can't be what their little conspiracy was standing up against. ep21 wrote: FUYUTSUKI: Where have you been for the past week? You're allowed to grieve, but you need to be aware that you are responsible for more than yourself now. IKARI: I know. Fuyutsuki, I am going to recommend a new project starting today. I have already proposed it to Chairman Kiel. FUYUTSUKI: You're not going to... IKARI: That's right. The path to godhood that none have ever succeeded in before. The Human Instrumentality Project.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Wed Oct 09, 2013 7:12 pm Gendo may just have worked out the specifics for the finalized plan (and not told SEELE certain details), just look at all their conversation about their "scenarios".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Oct 09, 2013 8:01 pm NemZ wrote: We know Yui & pals were explicitly against Seele's goal, but what that goal actually was is very hard to pin down. Remember that instrumentality is something Gendo suggests to them AFTER Yui has been absorbed and Fuyu is suprised by the move, so clearly that can't be what their little conspiracy was standing up against.

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Wed Oct 09, 2013 8:10 pm If Instrumentality wasn't the goal, what was Seele doing to cause 2I to begin with? Maybe he proposed the Unit-1-specific version?

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Wed Oct 09, 2013 9:28 pm Reichu wrote: Unless Gendo isn't actually implying that he came up with the idea, and is just saying that he's done something to bring it out of R&D and into actual action. Kind of like what Kendrix said, I guess. The way Gendo announces it seems to be with the expectation that Fuyutsuki already knows what he's talking about, i.e., it was a preexisting idea.

[]:             Quote Post by Falcon_of_the_Sun » Thu Oct 10, 2013 4:29 am My points, evidenced. They don't necessarily add to your elaborations, but possibly they can provide a more solid platform to base them. 1) From even before the Yui CE, her ideals/plan were different from Seele's, although she thought that keeping humanity in the dark about 2I and all was a necessary price to pay... Ep 21 - FUYUTSUKI (talking to Yui): I support your ideas, not Seele's. [...] YUI: Professor Fuyutsuki, it will be very dangerous to unleash that seal upon the world. 2) Yui's plan was to stick some souls into some Evas (I guess the Ikari family would get first dibs? mwahwahwah) Ep 26' - FUYUTSUKI: Humans create Evangelion in imitation of God... Is this our true goal? YUI: Yes. Humans can only live on this planet, but Evangelion can live forever... together with the human soul that dwells within it. Even after 5 billion years, when the Earth, the Moon, and even the Sun have disappeared, it will still exist as long as even one person still lives. It will be very lonely, but as long as that one person still lives... FUYUTSUKI: It will be the eternal proof that humankind has existed... 3) Whatever plan Yui had in mind, it was Gendo's too, see the use of OUR, although this might have changed once that he loses Eva-01 to Seele in EoE. Ep 24 - IKARI (talking to Eva-01): The time that has been given to us runs short. But the Lance of Longinus that stood in the way of our desire is no more. Soon, the final Angel will appear. If we destroy it, our wish will come true. Just a little longer, Yui. 4) Fuyu was never too sold on the idea, especially if it meant the disappearance of her beloved Yui. Also, Impacts were never his cup of tea, even in 2015. Ep 21 - FUYUTSUKI: But that's no reason for you to become an experimental subject. Ep 12 - IKARI: Yet this is a world that has been purified, purged of the original sin. FUYUTSUKI: I'd prefer a world where people live, no matter how stained with sin. Regarding Gendo proposing HIC to the Committee/Seele: chances are that they all (Seele and AEL/Gehirn) had it in mind, but Seele's primary objective at the tiime was to build Evas to fight the Angels. But then Yui's CE was the major ingredient in the HIC recipe, so once they got that, they thought it could be feasible and had to be started asap?

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Thu Oct 10, 2013 4:35 am You know, sometimes I feel that with all the grand plans about immortalising mankind, purifying the world and the rest, poor little Shinji and his personal issues should really be seen as a pretty irrelevant sideshow added as padding! (j/k)

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Oct 10, 2013 10:36 am You've got it completely backwards. (mised the j/k. Okay, Yui has it completely backwards) It's the other way around. Yui is hung up on an inevitable truth that healthy people just dismiss, or don't even think about, because they are irrelevant . In turn, she dismissed the most important things in favor of the abstract. Yes, mankind and all its works, even the stuff we left on the Moon, will eventually be wiped out when the sun explodes. Our own species will be extinct long before that. We've already got eternal markets out in space in the form of probes like Voyager, but they too will eventually collide with something and be destroyed, and even if by some miracle they were to float all the way out to the limits of space, they won't escape the heat death of the universe or the big crunch or Shiva unmaking reality or the Godhead waking up from the dream or what have you. So, Yui's monument isn't really eternal, either. It's an excuse for her to run away, and since we learn nothing of her personality or psychology, it's an excuse to abandon her husband and son for no apparent reason. If she was dying of an incurable disease, maybe, or had some reason she couldn't stay, that would be one thing, but we get nothing except Yui obviously putting her plan to float away forever, alone, ahead of her husband and her own offspring . There is no justification for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Oct 10, 2013 10:50 am Chuckman wrote: It's an excuse for her to run away, and since we learn nothing of her personality or psychology, it's an excuse to abandon her husband and son for no apparent reason. If she was dying of an incurable disease, maybe, or had some reason she couldn't stay, that would be one thing, but we get nothing except Yui obviously putting her plan to float away forever, alone, ahead of her husband and her own offspring . There is no justification for that.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Oct 10, 2013 12:12 pm FWIW, I posted my thoughts on the subject in the Impact Events thread. I have to keep reminding myself that I already worked it all out once, and there's no sense repeating myself every time the subject comes up. That thread was a lot of work, no need to do it twice!

[]:             Quote Post by Chuckman » Thu Oct 10, 2013 1:37 pm Reichu wrote: I did make a reply to you in the other thread, to which I don't think I got an answer. I bolded the most important part. It holds whether or not you want to give my musings about her becoming a Seed of Life the time of day. After all, some folks think that even a passive "monument" that lasts until the end of time would be a worthwhile thing. (To me, the idea of a sapient being floating in space forever is one of the most fucking depressing things I can think of and I can't possibly comprehend why a clone of a Seed of Life who has both fruits, Lilith's successor, and the Lilin's self-appointed legacy would condemn herself to such a fate if she could be out there continuing humanity instead. Especially being one of the central mother figures of the show.) Furthermore, "running away" implies that Yui wants to escape her family life for some reason, when the evidence seems to point in the exact opposite direction. It seems that your model of morality is based around the short-term and personal, with no room for the long-term and impersonal. Humans are fundamentally inclined toward the former, that much is true. However, dramatic action needs to take both kinds into account. It's because humans cannot easily detach themselves from "how does this affect me and my monkeysphere during the time I'll be alive?" that so many awful decisions are made by those in power today.

[]:             Quote Post by chaosakita » Mon Oct 14, 2013 8:31 pm Reichu wrote: Furthermore, "running away" implies that Yui wants to escape her family life for some reason, when the evidence seems to point in the exact opposite direction.

[]:             Quote Post by Noah » Sun Oct 20, 2013 11:34 pm Yui learns the plans of SEELE and finds it unfair that the future generation will have no choice in their own fate. Her main goal is a basic and motherly one, to create a better world for Shinji. Unfortunately, she does not know which world is better, one where SEELE wins or one where they fail. For this reason, she is motivated to protect Shinji and let him choose his own fate (and that of others). This is evidenced by Gendo's final scene in EoE where Yui is disappointed he has not done what's best for Shinji, which is what she wanted all along.

[]:             Quote Post by pen.pen » Tue Jan 07, 2014 4:40 pm Speaking of Yui, if her real plan was to put his soul into Eva 01... Did she really "forced" her son to watch her physical disappearance, while merging with a "Giant Robot"? (Assuming that it was not an accident)

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Tue Jan 07, 2014 4:44 pm pen.pen wrote: Did she really "forced" her son to watch her physical disappearance, while merging with a "Giant Robot"? (Assuming that it was not an accident)

[]:             Quote Post by SoryuUberAlles » Tue Jan 07, 2014 6:03 pm Reichu wrote: After all, some folks think that even a passive "monument" that lasts until the end of time would be a worthwhile thing. (To me, the idea of a sapient being floating in space forever is one of the most fucking depressing things I can think of ...

[]:             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Wed Oct 09, 2013 4:26 pm Yes, I see what you mean, I expressed myself very poorly sorry, what I wanted to ask is : YG
